title: plagiobothrys austinae
intro: Plagiobothrys austinae, also known as Western fetid marigold, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family, Boraginaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antibacterial agent. The health benefits of Plagiobothrys austinae include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Plagiobothrys austinae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal properties, although its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied.
